theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Health Care (+2.68%) and Utilities (+2.19%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.70%) and Consumer Discretionary (-0.56%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -1.09% versus +2.28% for defensives and +1.03% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained +1.14% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -0.80%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.55%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 2.04% to 15.81, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.07% to $4,191.30, crude oil was little changed at $68.51, and bitcoin gained 0.42% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
